title: Video Overlay Reverse Ffmpeg Macos
content_html:
[2025-08-14 14:00] User: How to overlay 2 videos to create a composite video. macOS, using free tools [2025-08-14 14:01] Assistant: Provided three options for overlaying videos:

  1. FFmpeg command-line method (powerful, free)
  2. iMovie GUI method (basic overlay)
  3. Shotcut GUI method (open-source, more control)

Included example FFmpeg commands for scaling, positioning, blending, and using transparency.

[2025-08-14 14:02] User: Both videos same size and 50% opacity [2025-08-14 14:03] Assistant: Gave FFmpeg blend filter command for 50% opacity: ffmpeg -i video1.mp4 -i video2.mp4 -filter_complex "[0:v][1:v]blend=all_mode=overlay:all_opacity=0.5" -c:a copy output.mp4 Also provided optional command for mixing audio.

[2025-08-14 14:04] User: Yes [2025-08-14 14:05] Assistant: Provided interactive-like FFmpeg command allowing opacity to be set via shell variable.

[2025-08-14 14:06] User: Yes [2025-08-14 14:07] Assistant: Gave Automator Quick Action script for macOS to blend two selected videos with adjustable opacity.

[2025-08-14 14:08] User: Can ffmpeg reverse a video? [2025-08-14 14:09] Assistant: Provided FFmpeg commands to reverse video only, reverse both video and audio, reverse without re-encoding, and reverse a clip section.

[2025-08-14 14:10] User: Yes [2025-08-14 14:11] Assistant: Provided FFmpeg command to reverse one of two overlay videos and blend forward and reverse versions with 50% opacity and mixed audio.

[2025-08-14 14:12] User: Yes [2025-08-14 14:13] Assistant: Provided full Automator Quick Action script to:

  • Reverse second video
  • Blend forward + reverse at adjustable opacity
  • Resize second video to match first
  • Mix forward + reversed audio
  • Save result in same folder Includes FFmpeg installation check and user input for opacity.

Main Scripts Included

  1. Blend Two Videos at 50% Opacity: ffmpeg -i video1.mp4 -i video2.mp4
    -filter_complex "[0:v][1:v]blend=all_mode=overlay:all_opacity=0.5"
    -c:a copy output.mp4

  2. Blend Two Videos with Adjustable Opacity (Shell Variable): OPACITY=0.5 && ffmpeg -i video1.mp4 -i video2.mp4
    -filter_complex "[0:v][1:v]blend=all_mode=overlay:all_opacity=${OPACITY}"
    -c:a copy output.mp4

  3. Automator Quick Action - Blend Two Videos with Adjustable Opacity:

  4. Reverse Video (Video Only): ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -vf reverse -af anull output.mp4

  5. Reverse Video + Audio: ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -vf reverse -af areverse output.mp4

  6. Forward + Reverse Composite Blend (FFmpeg): ffmpeg -i video1.mp4 -i video2.mp4
    -filter_complex "[1:v]reverse[rev]; [0:v][rev]blend=all_mode=overlay:all_opacity=0.5[v]; [0:a]volume=0.5[a0]; [1:a]areverse,volume=0.5[a1]; [a0][a1]amix=inputs=2:duration=longest[a]"
    -map "[v]" -map "[a]" output.mp4

  7. Automator Quick Action - Forward + Reverse Blend with Adjustable Opacity:
